title: nf-core/riboseq pipeline parameters
description: Analysis of ribosome profiling, or Ribo-seq (also named ribosome footprinting)

input_output_options.title: Input/output options
input_output_options.description: Define where the pipeline should find input data and save output data.

input.description: Path to comma-separated file containing information about the samples in the experiment.
input.help_text: You will need to create a design file with information about the samples in your experiment before running the pipeline. Use this parameter to specify its location. It has to be a comma-separated file with 3 columns, and a header row. See [usage docs](https://nf-co.re/riboseq/usage#samplesheet-input).

contrasts.description: A CSV file describing sample contrasts
contrasts.help_text: This file is used to define groups of samples from 'input' to compare.  It must contain at least the columns 'variable', 'reference' and 'target', and optionally a 'batch' column. 'variable' and 'batch' must be columns in the input sample sheet, 'reference' and 'target' are values in the column indicated by 'variable'.

outdir.description: The output directory where the results will be saved. You have to use absolute paths to storage on Cloud infrastructure.

email.help_text: Set this parameter to your e-mail address to get a summary e-mail with details of the run sent to you when the workflow exits. If set in your user config file (`~/.nextflow/config`) then you don't need to specify this on the command line for every run.

multiqc_title.description: MultiQC report title. Printed as page header, used for filename if not otherwise specified.

reference_genome_options.title: Reference genome options
reference_genome_options.description: Reference genome related files and options required for the workflow.

genome.help_text:
If using a reference genome configured in the pipeline using iGenomes (not recommended), use this parameter to give the ID for the reference. This is then used to build the full paths for all required reference genome files e.g. `--genome GRCh38`. 

See the [nf-core website docs](https://nf-co.re/usage/reference_genomes) for more details.

fasta.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--genome` is not specified. If you don't have the appropriate alignment index available this will be generated for you automatically. Combine with `--save_reference` to save alignment index for future runs.

gtf.help_text: This parameter is *mandatory* if `--genome` is not specified.

gff.help_text: This parameter must be specified if `--genome` or `--gtf` are not specified.

additional_fasta.description: FASTA file to concatenate to genome FASTA file e.g. containing spike-in sequences.
additional_fasta.help_text: If provided, the sequences in this file will get concatenated to the existing genome FASTA file, a GTF file will be automatically created using the entire sequence as the gene, transcript, and exon features, and any alignment index will get created from the combined FASTA and GTF. It is recommended to save the reference with `--save_reference` to re-use the index for future runs so you do not need to create it again.

star_index.description: Path to directory or tar.gz archive for pre-built STAR index.

salmon_index.description: Path to directory or tar.gz archive for pre-built Salmon index.

gencode.description: Specify if your GTF annotation is in GENCODE format.

gtf_extra_attributes.description: By default, the pipeline uses the `gene_name` field to obtain additional gene identifiers from the input GTF file when running Salmon.
gtf_extra_attributes.help_text: This behaviour can be modified by specifying `--gtf_extra_attributes` when running the pipeline. Note that you can also specify more than one desired value, separated by a comma e.g. `--gtf_extra_attributes gene_id,...`.

gtf_group_features.description: Define the attribute type used to group features in the GTF file when running Salmon.

igenomes_ignore.description: Do not load the iGenomes reference config.
igenomes_ignore.help_text: Do not load `igenomes.config` when running the pipeline. You may choose this option if you observe clashes between custom parameters and those supplied in `igenomes.config`.

igenomes_base.description: The base path to the igenomes reference files

read_trimming_options.title: Read trimming options
read_trimming_options.description: Options to adjust read trimming criteria.

trimmer.description: Specifies the trimming tool to use - available options are 'trimgalore' and 'fastp'.

extra_trimgalore_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to Trim Galore! command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

extra_fastp_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to fastp command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

min_trimmed_reads.description: Minimum number of trimmed reads below which samples are removed from further processing. Some downstream steps in the pipeline will fail if this threshold is too low.

read_filtering_options.title: Read filtering options
read_filtering_options.description: Options for filtering reads prior to alignment

extra_fqlint_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to the fq lint command.

contaminant_fasta.description: FASTA file containing sequences for rRNA (cytoplasmic/mitochondrial), tRNA, snRNA/snoRNA and other contaminants to remove prior to alignment.
contaminant_fasta.help_text: Provide a single FASTA file that aggregates all contaminant references you would like to screen against. Include at minimum cytoplasmic and mitochondrial rRNA plus highly abundant tRNA loci for Ribo-seq.

filter_aligner.description: Aligner to use for contaminant filtering.

bowtie_index.description: Path to directory or tar.gz archive for pre-built Bowtie contaminant index.
bowtie_index.help_text: The Bowtie index will be generated automatically from `--contaminant_fasta` if not provided and `--save_reference` is enabled for re-use.

bowtie2_index.description: Path to directory or tar.gz archive for pre-built Bowtie2 contaminant index.
bowtie2_index.help_text: The Bowtie2 index will be generated automatically from `--contaminant_fasta` when `--filter_aligner bowtie2` is selected.

save_contaminant_reads.description: Save reads classified as contaminants by Bowtie/Bowtie2.

umi_options.title: UMI options
umi_options.description: Options for processing reads with unique molecular identifiers

umi_dedup_tool.description: Specifies the tool to use for UMI deduplication - available options are 'umitools' and 'umicollapse'.

umitools_extract_method.description: UMI pattern to use. Can be either 'string' (default) or 'regex'.
umitools_extract_method.help_text: More details can be found in the [UMI-tools documentation](https://umi-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/extract.html#extract-method).

umitools_bc_pattern.help_text: More details can be found in the [UMI-tools documentation](https://umi-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/extract.html#extract-method).
umitools_bc_pattern.description: The UMI barcode pattern to use e.g. 'NNNNNN' indicates that the first 6 nucleotides of the read are from the UMI.

umitools_bc_pattern2.description: The UMI barcode pattern to use if the UMI is located in read 2.

umi_discard_read.description: After UMI barcode extraction discard either R1 or R2 by setting this parameter to 1 or 2, respectively.

umitools_umi_separator.description: The character that separates the UMI in the read name. Most likely a colon if you skipped the extraction with UMI-tools and used other software.

umitools_grouping_method.description: Method to use to determine read groups by subsuming those with similar UMIs. All methods start by identifying the reads with the same mapping position, but treat similar yet nonidentical UMIs differently.

umitools_dedup_stats.help_text: It can be quite time consuming generating these output stats - see [#827](https://github.com/nf-core/rnaseq/issues/827).
umitools_dedup_stats.description: Generate output stats when running "umi_tools dedup".

alignment_options.title: Alignment options
alignment_options.description: Options to adjust parameters and filtering criteria for read alignments.

aligner.description: Specifies the alignment algorithm to use - available options are currently 'star' and 'hisat2'.

pseudo_aligner_kmer_size.description: Kmer length passed to indexing step of pseudoaligners
pseudo_aligner_kmer_size.help_text: Failure to set a good kmer size could cause issues with quantification with Kallisto or Salmon. This is mostly an issue for short reads (<50bp), where the default kmer size of 31 is an problem.

bam_csi_index.description: Create a CSI index for BAM files instead of the traditional BAI index. This will be required for genomes with larger chromosome sizes.

star_ignore_sjdbgtf.description: When using pre-built STAR indices do not re-extract and use splice junctions from the GTF file.

salmon_quant_libtype.description: Override Salmon library type inferred based on strandedness defined in meta object.

min_mapped_reads.description: Minimum percentage of uniquely mapped reads below which samples are removed from further processing.
min_mapped_reads.help_text: Some downstream steps in the pipeline will fail if this threshold is too low.

seq_center.description: Sequencing center information to be added to read group of BAM files.

extra_star_align_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to STAR alignment command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline. Only available for the STAR-Salmon route.

star_out_tmpdir.description: Base directory for STAR temporary files (passed as --outTmpDir). Use a Linux/Posix filesystem that supports FIFO (avoid NTFS/FAT mounts). A unique subdirectory is created per task.

extra_salmon_quant_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to Salmon quant command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

stranded_threshold.description: The fraction of stranded reads that must be assigned to a strandedness for confident assignment. Must be at least 0.5.

unstranded_threshold.description: The difference in fraction of stranded reads assigned to 'forward' and 'reverse' below which a sample is classified as 'unstranded'. By default the forward and reverse fractions must differ by less than 0.1 for the sample to be called as unstranded.

riboseq_specific_options.title: Riboseq-specific options
riboseq_specific_options.description: Options related to riboseq-specific analysis modules

sorf_filter.description: Filter BAM files before running sORF prediction tools.
sorf_filter.help_text: Applies to all sORF prediction tools. Filtering is intended to enforce unique mapping, exclude unwanted contigs (e.g. mitochondrial/chloroplast/unlocalized scaffolds), and keep a footprint length window (default 28-30).

sorf_unique_mode.description: How to define uniquely-mapped reads for sORF filtering.
sorf_unique_mode.help_text: Supported modes: auto (prefer NH tag when present; otherwise use MAPQ threshold), nh (require NH==1), mapq (require MAPQ>=sorf_unique_mapq).

sorf_unique_mapq.description: MAPQ threshold used when sorf_unique_mode is 'mapq' (or in 'auto' mode when NH is not available).

sorf_read_len_min.description: Minimum read length to keep (inclusive) when filtering for sORF prediction.

sorf_read_len_max.description: Maximum read length to keep (inclusive) when filtering for sORF prediction.

sorf_exclude_contigs_regex.description: Regex of reference contig names to exclude before sORF prediction.
sorf_exclude_contigs_regex.help_text: Default covers common Gencode/UCSC-style mitochondrial contigs (chrM/MT) and Ensembl Plants-style organelle contigs (Mt/Pt), plus typical unlocalized scaffolds (chrUn_*) and alt/random/fix contigs. You may need to customize this for your reference genome. Set to an empty string to disable contig-name exclusion.

sorf_predict_pooled.description: Run pooled (all-samples) sORF prediction in addition to per-sample prediction.
sorf_predict_pooled.help_text: Disabled by default for performance. This only affects sORF prediction steps (QC can remain per-sample).

extra_ribotish_quality_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to the ribotish quality command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

extra_ribotish_predict_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to the ribotish predict command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

extra_ribotricer_prepareorfs_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to the ribotricer prepare-orfs command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

extra_ribotricer_detectorfs_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to the ribotricer detect-orfs command in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

extra_anota2seq_run_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to anota2seq in addition to defaults defined by the pipeline.

skip_unify_orf_predictions.description: Skip unified ORF predictions across tools.

unify_orf_predictions_prefix.description: Output prefix for unified ORF predictions (base name only).

unify_orf_min_len.description: Minimum ORF length (amino acids) for unified predictions.

extra_unify_orf_predictions_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to unify_orf_predictions.py.

orf_classify_output_dir.description: Output directory name for ORF classification results (relative to results).

orf_classify_ensembl_dir.description: Ensembl annotation directory for gencode classification mode.

extra_orf_classify_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to classify_orfs_wrapper.py.

optional_outputs.title: Optional outputs
optional_outputs.description: Additional output files produces as intermediates that can be saved

save_merged_fastq.description: Save FastQ files after merging re-sequenced libraries in the results directory.

save_umi_intermeds.description: If this option is specified, intermediate FastQ and BAM files produced by UMI-tools are also saved in the results directory.

save_contaminant_reads.description: Save FastQ files containing reads filtered out as contaminants.

save_reference.description: If generated by the pipeline save the STAR index in the results directory.
save_reference.help_text: If an alignment index is generated by the pipeline use this parameter to save it to your results folder. These can then be used for future pipeline runs, reducing processing times.

save_trimmed.description: Save the trimmed FastQ files in the results directory.
save_trimmed.help_text: By default, trimmed FastQ files will not be saved to the results directory. Specify this flag (or set to true in your config file) to copy these files to the results directory when complete.

save_align_intermeds.description: Save the intermediate BAM files from the alignment step.
save_align_intermeds.help_text: By default, intermediate BAM files will not be saved. The final BAM files created after the appropriate filtering step are always saved to limit storage usage. Set this parameter to also save other intermediate BAM files.

save_unaligned.description: Where possible, save unaligned reads from either STAR, HISAT2 or Salmon to the results directory.
save_unaligned.help_text: This may either be in the form of FastQ or BAM files depending on the options available for that particular tool.

process_skipping_options.title: Process skipping options
process_skipping_options.description: Options to skip various steps within the workflow.

skip_gtf_filter.description: Skip filtering of GTF for valid scaffolds and/ or transcript IDs.
skip_gtf_filter.help_text: If you're confident on the validity of the GTF with respect to the genome fasta file, or wish to disregard failures thriggered by the filtering module, activate this option.

skip_gtf_transcript_filter.description: Skip the 'transcript_id' checking component of the GTF filtering script used in the pipeline.

skip_umi_extract.description: Skip the UMI extraction from the read in case the UMIs have been moved to the headers in advance of the pipeline run.

skip_linting.description: Skip linting checks during FASTQ preprocessing and filtering.

skip_trimming.help_text: Use this if your input FastQ files have already been trimmed outside of the workflow or if you're very confident that there is no adapter contamination in your data.

skip_alignment.description: Skip all of the alignment-based processes within the pipeline.

skip_qc.description: Skip all QC steps except for MultiQC.

extra_riboseqc_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to RiboseQC analysis.

skip_orfquant.description: Skip ORFquant ORF detection and quantification. Note: ORFquant requires RiboseQC to be enabled.

extra_orfquant_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to ORFquant (e.g., 'plot_results=TRUE' to generate plots).

run_prefilter_qc.description: Run QC analysis on unfiltered BAMs (with MT reads) for comparison with postfilter results. Increases runtime and storage requirements.
run_prefilter_qc.help_text: By default, only postfilter QC (MT-removed BAMs) is performed. Enable this to also run prefilter QC for comparison purposes.

institutional_config_options.title: Institutional config options
institutional_config_options.description: Parameters used to describe centralised config profiles. These should not be edited.
institutional_config_options.help_text: The centralised nf-core configuration profiles use a handful of pipeline parameters to describe themselves. This information is then printed to the Nextflow log when you run a pipeline. You should not need to change these values when you run a pipeline.

custom_config_version.description: Git commit id for Institutional configs.

custom_config_base.help_text: If you're running offline, Nextflow will not be able to fetch the institutional config files from the internet. If you don't need them, then this is not a problem. If you do need them, you should download the files from the repo and tell Nextflow where to find them with this parameter.

test_data_base.description: Base path / URL for data used in the test profiles
test_data_base.help_text: Warning: The `-profile test` samplesheet file itself contains remote paths. Setting this parameter does not alter the contents of that file.

generic_options.title: Generic options
generic_options.description: Less common options for the pipeline, typically set in a config file.
generic_options.help_text:
These options are common to all nf-core pipelines and allow you to customise some of the core preferences for how the pipeline runs.

Typically these options would be set in a Nextflow config file loaded for all pipeline runs, such as `~/.nextflow/config`.

publish_dir_mode.description: Method used to save pipeline results to output directory.
publish_dir_mode.help_text: The Nextflow `publishDir` option specifies which intermediate files should be saved to the output directory. This option tells the pipeline what method should be used to move these files. See [Nextflow docs](https://www.nextflow.io/docs/latest/process.html#publishdir) for details.

email_on_fail.description: Email address for completion summary, only when pipeline fails.
email_on_fail.help_text: An email address to send a summary email to when the pipeline is completed - ONLY sent if the pipeline does not exit successfully.

plaintext_email.description: Send plain-text email instead of HTML.

max_multiqc_email_size.description: File size limit when attaching MultiQC reports to summary emails.

monochrome_logs.description: Do not use coloured log outputs.

hook_url.description: Incoming hook URL for messaging service
hook_url.help_text: Incoming hook URL for messaging service. Currently, MS Teams and Slack are supported.

multiqc_config.description: Custom config file to supply to MultiQC.

multiqc_logo.description: Custom logo file to supply to MultiQC. File name must also be set in the MultiQC config file

multiqc_methods_description.description: Custom MultiQC yaml file containing HTML including a methods description.

validate_params.description: Boolean whether to validate parameters against the schema at runtime

pipelines_testdata_base_path.description: Base URL or local path to location of pipeline test dataset files

trace_report_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the trace report filename. Default is the date and time in the format yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss.
